description_long: This object is a small, round, bushy tree with a dense, spherical canopy composed of numerous overlapping, dark green leaves. The foliage appears to be arranged in a layered, almost cloud-like fashion, with the leaves forming a thick, impenetrable mass that obscures the trunk and branches from most angles. The tree's silhouette is irregular but generally circular, with some jagged edges created by protruding leaves. There are subtle hints of lighter green highlights scattered throughout the canopy, suggesting a mix of new and mature foliage. The trunk is only faintly visible at the base, indicating it is relatively thin compared to the overall canopy size. This type of tree is commonly found in urban parks, streetscapes, or landscaped gardens, providing shade and visual interest.
description: A small, round, bushy tree with a dense, dark green leafy canopy and a barely visible thin trunk. The foliage forms a thick, spherical mass with jagged edges.
description_view_0: From this view, the tree appears as a nearly perfect dark green sphere with dense, overlapping leaves and minimal trunk visibility.
description_view_1: This angle shows a slightly more irregular silhouette, with some leaves jutting out, but the canopy remains dense and spherical.
description_view_2: The tree maintains its round, bushy appearance, with the foliage forming a thick, dark mass and only a hint of the trunk at the base.
description_view_3: This view highlights the dense, cloud-like arrangement of the leaves, with a few lighter green patches visible within the dark canopy.
